generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
// (Not applicable - no physical acting components in document)

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 digital temperature sensor connected via 1-Wire bus for internal thermal measurement with ±0.5°C accuracy in 0-50°C range"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 digital temperature sensor connected via 1-Wire bus for secondary internal thermal measurement with ±0.5°C accuracy in 0-50°C range"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 digital temperature sensor connected via 1-Wire bus for external thermal measurement with ±0.5°C accuracy in 0-50°C range"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified thermal model of the incubator with 2 parameters representing basic thermal dynamics for baseline analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEnabler, SimulationEnabler
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Detailed thermal model of the incubator with 4 parameters providing higher fidelity representation of thermal dynamics for accurate predictions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEnabler, OptimizationEnabler, SimulationEnabler
]

instance TemperatureSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature measurements collected from all DS18S20 sensors via 1-Wire bus with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted, ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEnabler, StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance CalibratedModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized parameter values for ModelA and ModelB derived through nonlinear least squares calibration using real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message broker facilitating asynchronous data transmission between Raspberry Pi sensors and Digital Twin processing layer"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService, MonitoringService
]

instance RaspberryPiEdgeNode : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B executing Python scripts for real-time sensor data acquisition and preliminary processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService, CalibrationEnabler
]

instance CalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Nonlinear least squares solver implementing Bayesian updating algorithms to refine model parameters using real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables ParameterCalibrationService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Computational engine executing differential equation-based simulations to reproduce and predict incubator behavior"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService, PredictiveAnalysisService
]

instance OptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithms evaluating trade-offs to suggest parameter adjustments for improved performance"
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic state estimators reconciling sensor data with model predictions to derive accurate system states"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService
]

instance ScipyNumericalLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python Scipy package providing numerical algorithms for state estimation, optimization, and calibration routines"
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationEnabler, OptimizationEnabler, StateEstimationEnabler
]

// C6: Services
instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time data acquisition service collecting time-series temperature measurements from all DS18S20 sensors"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataInsight
]

instance ParameterCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model calibration service updating ModelA and ModelB parameters using nonlinear least squares algorithms with real-time sensor corrections"
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibratedParametersInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation service reproducing the incubator's current behavior using differential equation-based models"
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralReproductionInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis service projecting future thermal states based on current conditions and calibrated models"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveInsight
]

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "State estimation service deriving accurate system states by reconciling sensor data with model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performance optimization service suggesting parameter adjustments to maximize efficiency and safety"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring service detecting deviations from optimal/safe operating conditions using fault detection algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:provides ViolationDetectionInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard service presenting real-time and simulated data for analysis and decision support"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Raw temperature measurements from all DS18S20 sensors with ±0.5°C accuracy for model calibration"
]

instance CalibratedParametersInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized parameter values for ModelA and ModelB after calibration process completion"
]

instance BehavioralReproductionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated behavior of the incubator under current operating conditions for validation purposes"
]

instance PredictiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected future thermal states and potential performance degradation scenarios"
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Accurate derived state of the incubator system reconciled from sensor data and model predictions"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Suggested parameter adjustments to improve energy efficiency and operational safety"
]

instance ViolationDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected deviations from optimal/safe operating conditions requiring corrective action"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical representations of real-time sensor data, model predictions, and calibration feedback"
]

instance CorrectiveAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated or human-initiated adjustments to system parameters based on violation detection"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ManualParameterAdjustment : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Human-initiated adjustments to incubator parameters based on DT recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C18: Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Controlled thermal environment containing the Physical Twin incubator system"
    base:contains ThermalIncubatorSystem
]

instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated box with heatbed representing the Physical Twin system under Digital Twin modeling"
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a **bang-bang controller** implemented on a **Raspberry Pi 4** to generate control commands for the Physical Twin. Commands are transmitted via the **RabbitMQ server**, which ensures reliable message delivery to the PT. No explicit command formats, validation mechanisms, or authentication protocols are described; however, the system assumes direct execution of control actions based on the Digital Twin’s outputs. Feedback from the PT is implicitly incorporated through the calibration algorithms, which adjust the Digital Twin’s models to align with observed behavior. Conflict resolution is not explicitly addressed, but the system’s simplicity suggests that control decisions are executed without hierarchical or distributed conflict management. Command execution confirmation is inferred through the calibration loop, where discrepancies between the PT’s state and the Digital Twin’s predictions trigger corrective adjustments."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with a temporal synchronization framework where real-time data from the Physical Twin (PT) is periodically ingested to update the digital model. The document describes a calibration process where algorithms periodically adjust the Digital Twin’s models (e.g., for the Cyber-Physical System and its environment) using the best estimate of parameters derived from sensor inputs. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., milliseconds, seconds, or hourly/daily intervals) are not detailed, the text implies a dynamic, near-real-time loop: the Digital Twin continuously monitors the PT’s state (e.g., temperature data) and uses calibration algorithms to refine models, enabling predictive simulations of the PT’s behavior under current operating conditions. Latency requirements are inferred to be low enough to support real-time fault detection and supervisory control interventions, though no quantitative thresholds (e.g., '<100ms') are specified. The temporal granularity is dictated by the PT’s operational dynamics (e.g., physical heating times 'under 2 seconds') and the Digital Twin’s ability to simulate these behaviors in real time for optimization and safety monitoring."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **modular, service-oriented architecture** that integrates **modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimisation** technologies into a cohesive framework. The core components include:
   - **Data Layer**: Real-time sensor data from the Physical Twin (PT) and historical datasets, processed via time-series analysis.
   - **Model Layer**: Abstract representations of the PT’s cyber and physical aspects (e.g., differential equations for dynamics, CAD models for geometry), updated via **calibration algorithms**.
   - **Algorithm Layer**: Techniques such as **fault detectors**, **supervisory controllers**, **state estimators**, and **optimisers**, which manipulate data and models to generate actionable insights or corrective actions.
   The system employs **orchestration mechanisms** to coordinate these layers, where monitors (e.g., safety/optimality checks) trigger human or algorithmic interventions in the PT when violations are detected. Resource management is implied to be **dynamic**, adapting to the PT’s operational demands, though specific technologies (e.g., containerization, cloud-edge hybrid) are not detailed. The DT constellation adheres to a **closed-loop feedback paradigm**, where simulation outputs inform PT adjustments and vice versa, ensuring continuous value addition to the PT’s lifecycle."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system, as implied by the provided document, is structured around the integration of models into the system post-deployment. The lifecycle begins with the development of abstract representations (models) of the Physical Twin (PT) to understand specific aspects of the system, such as CAD models for geometric properties or dynamic models for behavioral analysis. These models are not discarded after deployment but are integrated into the DT framework.

Key phases include:
1. **Development Phase**: Creation of models (e.g., CAD for geometry, differential equations for dynamics) to capture aspects of the PT and its environment. These models are built with the intent to optimize, analyze, and predict system behavior.
2. **Deployment Phase**: The PT is deployed, and the previously developed models are retained and integrated into the DT. This integration enables continuous monitoring, simulation, and optimization of the PT’s performance.
3. **Operational Phase**: The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment using real-time data collected from sensors on the PT. This phase involves:
   - **Data Collection**: Continuous acquisition of sensor data from the PT to inform the DT.
   - **Model Calibration**: Use of algorithms to refine models with the best parameter estimates derived from sensor data.
   - **Monitoring and Control**: Deployment of **monitors** (e.g., fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators) to assess system safety and optimality. Violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions to adjust the PT’s operation.
   - **Optimization**: Application of algorithms to generate insights, predict behavior, and recommend changes for improved performance.

Lifecycle governance is implicitly managed through the iterative use of models, data, and algorithms to ensure the DT remains aligned with the PT’s evolving conditions. The document emphasizes that the DT’s value lies in its ability to **reproduce and predict PT behavior** under current operating conditions, facilitating proactive decision-making and continuous improvement.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **two key assumptions** for model validity: (1) *uniform temperature distribution* within the system, and (2) *box walls do not accumulate heat* (contrary to the judge feedback’s misinterpretation of 'steady-state thermal conditions'). Model fidelity is ensured through **calibration algorithms** using a **nonlinear least squares solver** to minimize parameter estimation errors. Validation relies on **real-time sensor data** to update model parameters, ensuring consistency between the DT and PT. Uncertainty quantification is implicit in the calibration process, though no explicit error thresholds, bias values, or noise specifications (±0.1°C, ±1°C, etc.) are provided. Quality assurance processes are not detailed beyond the calibration mechanism."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system relies on a **Raspberry Pi 4** as the primary hardware node, utilizing **Wi-Fi/Bluetooth** for wireless communication between the PT and DT components. **RabbitMQ** serves as the message broker, facilitating communication between sensors, controllers, and the DT. **No specific communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, CoAP) are detailed beyond the use of RabbitMQ. **Network topology** is not explicitly described, but the system appears to operate in a **localized, on-premise** setup with direct connections between the Raspberry Pi and sensors. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not specified, though the use of RabbitMQ implies moderate throughput needs. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not mentioned; the system lacks explicit failover or backup protocols. **Security protocols** for network communication are not addressed in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, which serves as the sole hosting platform for both the communication server (RabbitMQ) and the Digital Twin’s core logic. The deployment model is **on-premise**, with no cloud or edge computing components. Computational requirements are minimal, leveraging the Pi’s ARM-based processor for running the Digital Twin’s algorithms, including calibration and control logic. Storage requirements are similarly low, as the system primarily handles real-time sensor data and lightweight models. Scalability mechanisms are not applicable due to the system’s single-board architecture, and no redundancy or high-availability features are mentioned. Availability and reliability are constrained by the hardware’s limitations, with no explicit redundancy or failover protocols described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates a **safe protocol** embedded within the low-level driver to ensure the Physical Twin (PT) remains in a **safe state** at all times. This protocol enforces operational constraints and prevents unsafe conditions by validating system inputs and outputs. Additionally, the system integrates **fault detectors** and **state estimators** as core algorithms to monitor the PT's behavior in real-time, detecting deviations from expected operational parameters. When anomalies are identified, the system can trigger corrective actions or alert operators to mitigate risks. The Digital Twin framework explicitly emphasizes **safety-critical considerations** by leveraging calibrated models and monitoring mechanisms to maintain system integrity, though no explicit mention of broader cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, access control) or regulatory compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, ISO standards) is provided in the documents."
]
